$comment: Phase 2.3 authoring source — per-regulation content fills closing tier-1 clause gaps for DORA, ISO/IEC 27001:2022, SOC 2 2017 TSC, PCI-DSS v4.0, and SOX-ITGC PCAOB AS 2201. Generated by scripts/_bootstrap_phase2_3_data.py. Consumed by scripts/generate_phase2_3_per_regulation.py.

subcat_titles.22.11: PCI DSS v4.0 — per-regulation content fill (Phase 2.3)

new_ucs.title: DORA Art.6 — ICT risk-management framework evidence: control catalogue drift detection
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT assess control design effectiveness; only detects drift between the documented control catalogue and operational control inventory in the governance platform (Archer, ServiceNow GRC, etc.).
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'dora_art6_control_catalogue_drift' (daily) produces the delta of documented vs operational controls, counts of orphaned controls, and the list of ICT assets whose controlFamily is not in the approved catalogue; routed to the compliance_summary index.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.6 requires a 'sound, comprehensive and well-documented ICT risk-management framework'. A continuously-verified delta between documented and operational controls is the direct evidence a supervisor would request.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.1 policies for information security — contributing evidence that the policy framework is operational, but ISO requires additional management-review artefacts.
NIST 800-53 PM-9 (Risk Management Strategy) — contributes by proving the strategy's control mapping is current.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Deleting a control in the GRC tool (state=inactive) that is still listed in dora_control_catalogue.csv causes the UC to fire within 24h naming the specific control_id and ict_service.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A newly-added control whose family is present in the approved catalogue and whose state is active does NOT fire.

new_ucs.dataSources: `index=grc` sourcetype=archer:control, sourcetype=servicenow:grc_control; `index=cmdb` sourcetype=cmdb:ci_ict_service; controlFamily catalogue lookup `dora_control_catalogue.csv`.
new_ucs.app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow
new_ucs.spl:
index=grc sourcetype IN (archer:control,servicenow:grc_control) earliest=-1d
| stats latest(control_family) AS control_family latest(state) AS state BY control_id ict_service
| lookup dora_control_catalogue.csv control_family OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved) OR state="inactive"
| table ict_service control_id control_family state approved
new_ucs.description: Compares the live control inventory from the governance platform against the Board-approved DORA control catalogue (per Art.6 requirement for a documented ICT risk-management framework). Surfaces orphan controls (in operations but not in catalogue) and decayed controls (in catalogue but now inactive).
new_ucs.value: Replaces the annual 'do we still have the framework' attestation with a continuous reconciliation — regulators under DORA Art.6 expect the framework to be 'soundly, comprehensively and well-documented', meaning drift must be detected and remediated, not just discovered at the next audit.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Export control inventory daily from Archer/ServiceNow GRC into Splunk; (2) maintain dora_control_catalogue.csv as the Board-approved baseline with approval date and approver; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) drift >0 opens a risk-register entry assigned to the CISO with 30-day remediation SLA; (5) quarterly sign-off by the ICT risk committee.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of drifted controls, timechart of drift count by day, single value 'days since last zero-drift state'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Catalogue refreshes legitimately introduce new control_family values until the GRC platform syncs — maintain a 24h grace window in the lookup's effective-from date.

new_ucs.title: DORA Art.7 — ICT systems inventory completeness: unmanaged endpoints attached to financial services
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT test the operating-system patch posture (covered by separate vulnerability-management UCs); detects only the presence of hosts handling financial-service traffic that are absent from the ICT asset inventory.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'dora_art7_unmanaged_endpoint' (hourly) outputs per-host diagnostic record: asset_id (if any), hostname, observed_financial_service, inventory_gap_reason, to the compliance_summary index.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.7 requires ICT systems, protocols and tools to be 'reliable' and 'technologically up to date' with an up-to-date inventory; an unmanaged host attached to payments is the direct failure mode this UC detects.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.9 (Inventory of information and other associated assets) — same control objective, directly evidenced.
NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) asset-management subclause is directly supported.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Simulating an authentication by a non-inventoried host against the trading app causes the UC to fire within the hour naming the hostname and inventory_gap_reason='not in ICT asset inventory'.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A fully-inventoried host performing the same authentication does NOT fire.

new_ucs.dataSources: `index=endpoint` sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security / sourcetype=linux:audit; `index=cmdb` sourcetype=cmdb:ci_server; authoritative financial-service CMDB view `financial_services_servers.csv`.
new_ucs.app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows, Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux
new_ucs.spl:
| tstats summariesonly=t count FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE Authentication.app IN ("payments","trading","settlement") BY Authentication.dest
| rename Authentication.dest AS hostname
| lookup financial_services_servers.csv hostname OUTPUT asset_id owner team
| where isnull(asset_id)
| eval inventory_gap_reason="not in ICT asset inventory"
| table hostname inventory_gap_reason count
new_ucs.description: Uses the CIM Authentication data model to find hosts that have been seen handling financial-service traffic but are absent from the official ICT asset CMDB. Under DORA Art.7 the financial entity must maintain 'up-to-date inventories' of ICT systems; a shadow endpoint with access to payments/trading/settlement is a direct Art.7 non-conformance.
new_ucs.value: Turns the quarterly 'asset inventory reconciliation' into a continuous signal, closing the time window during which an unmanaged host can process financial transactions unnoticed.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Onboard auth data to CIM Authentication DM; (2) maintain financial_services_servers.csv from the ICT asset register nightly; (3) schedule UC hourly; (4) hit opens a ServiceNow CMDB task to register the host; (5) SLA: resolve within 72h or isolate; (6) exclude build/imaging windows with an effective-from allowlist.
new_ucs.visualization: Bar chart of inventory gaps by financial service, table of hosts with count, single value 'hosts in gap'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Short-lived CI/CD build runners used for payments tests may legitimately appear off-inventory — allow-list by hostname-pattern in financial_services_servers.csv with a 24h TTL.

new_ucs.title: DORA Art.8 — ICT risk identification: newly discovered high-severity exposure on critical financial services
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT replace the scanner; consumes the scanner's output and prioritises findings on the subset of hosts classified as 'critical financial service' per DORA Art.8(1).
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'dora_art8_new_crit_exposure' (every 15m) emits, per finding: finding_id, ict_service, cvss, cve, discovered_time, registration_sla_met (boolean), to the compliance_summary index.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.8 requires continuous identification of ICT-related risks; a 15-minute detection + 24h register SLA directly maps to that obligation for critical services.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.8.8 Management of technical vulnerabilities — directly evidenced.
NIST 800-53 RA-5 Vulnerability Scanning — contributes; monitoring here is a scanner consumer.
PCI-DSS 11.3.1 internal vulnerability scans; this UC reuses the same scanner feed to detect material exposures.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Creating a test finding with CVSS=8 on a host tagged criticality_tier=critical causes the UC to fire within 15m naming the CVE and ict_service.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A CVSS=5 or non-critical host finding does NOT fire.

new_ucs.dataSources: `index=vm` sourcetype=tenable:sc:vuln OR sourcetype=qualys:host; `index=cmdb` sourcetype=cmdb:ci_ict_service; DORA critical-service lookup `dora_critical_services.csv`.
new_ucs.app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Add-on for Tenable, Splunk Add-on for Qualys
new_ucs.spl:
index=vm sourcetype IN (tenable:sc:vuln,qualys:host) cvss>=7 earliest=-15m
| rename dest AS hostname
| lookup dora_critical_services.csv hostname OUTPUT ict_service criticality_tier
| where criticality_tier="critical"
| eval registration_sla_met=if(relative_time(now(),"-24h")<=_time,1,0)
| stats count BY ict_service cvss cve hostname registration_sla_met
new_ucs.description: Identifies newly-discovered CVSS>=7 exposures on hosts supporting a DORA-critical financial service. Measures whether the finding is registered in the ICT risk register within the 24h target implied by Art.8 'continuous identification'.
new_ucs.value: Produces per-finding evidence that critical-service exposure is identified and logged within the DORA-aligned window, rather than relying on monthly scanner review meetings.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Onboard scanner output; (2) tag hosts with ict_service + criticality_tier nightly; (3) schedule UC every 15m; (4) each hit opens a risk-register entry (ServiceNow) within 24h; (5) weekly report to ICT risk committee; (6) breach of 24h SLA escalates to the Head of IR and Board/Audit Committee.
new_ucs.visualization: Timechart of new criticals per ICT service, table of unresolved findings, single value 'findings past 24h SLA'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Scanner re-baselines after signature updates can produce a one-time spike of 're-discovered' findings — cross-reference against first_seen and exclude.

new_ucs.title: DORA Art.17 — ICT incident classification timeliness: major-incident clock evidence
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT classify whether an incident is 'major' (that is a human decision by the incident commander); detects only the elapsed time between detection, initial, intermediate, and final notification fields in the incident record.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'dora_art17_incident_clock' (every 5m) produces per-incident timing: detected_at, classified_at, notified_at, notification_intermediate_at, notification_final_at, SLA_breached fields; routed to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.17(1) requires an ICT-related incident management process; Art.19(4) + the RTS impose the 24h/72h/30d notification clock this UC measures.
NIS2 Art.23 incident notification — the same clock structure applies, so this UC double-counts for both frameworks in financial entities subject to both.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.24 Information security incident management planning and preparation — directly evidenced.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Injecting a synthetic major_incident with detected_at=-25h and no classified_at sets SLA_breached=1 on the next 5-min cycle.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A major incident with detect→classify in 4h, classify→initial in 10h, initial→intermediate in 30h, intermediate→final in 15d returns SLA_breached=0.

new_ucs.dataSources: `index=soar` sourcetype=servicenow:sir_incident OR sourcetype=phantom:incident; `index=ticketing` sourcetype=jira:issue (IR project); regulator notification log `index=compliance` sourcetype=dora:notification.
new_ucs.app: Splunk SOAR, Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow
new_ucs.spl:
index=soar sourcetype IN (servicenow:sir_incident,phantom:incident) tag::major_incident=true earliest=-1d
| stats min(detected_at) AS detected_at min(classified_at) AS classified_at min(notified_at) AS notified_at min(intermediate_at) AS intermediate_at min(final_at) AS final_at BY incident_id
| eval detect_to_classify_h=round((classified_at-detected_at)/3600,2)
| eval classify_to_initial_h=round((notified_at-classified_at)/3600,2)
| eval initial_to_inter_h=round((intermediate_at-notified_at)/3600,2)
| eval inter_to_final_h=round((final_at-intermediate_at)/3600,2)
| eval SLA_breached=if(detect_to_classify_h>24 OR classify_to_initial_h>24 OR initial_to_inter_h>72 OR inter_to_final_h>720,1,0)
| table incident_id detected_at classified_at notified_at intermediate_at final_at detect_to_classify_h classify_to_initial_h initial_to_inter_h inter_to_final_h SLA_breached
new_ucs.description: Measures, per major ICT incident, the elapsed time between detection, classification, initial notification, intermediate notification, and final report. Any SLA breach against the DORA Art.17 + ESA RTS timeline (24h initial / 72h intermediate / 30d final) sets SLA_breached=1 and triggers a finding.
new_ucs.value: Supervisors under DORA request the per-incident clock evidence. This UC produces it automatically, so the entity can prove the notification workflow was operated within the required windows or identify the exact root cause of a miss.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Normalise detected_at/classified_at/notified_at/intermediate_at/final_at in the IR tool; (2) tag major incidents at classification; (3) schedule UC every 5m; (4) SLA_breached=1 escalates to CISO + Head of IR; (5) monthly roll-up to Board; (6) annual tabletop exercise validates clock fidelity.
new_ucs.visualization: Per-incident Gantt of phase durations, timechart of breaches per quarter, single value 'open incidents at risk of SLA breach in next 4h'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Incidents re-classified as non-major after initial raise can show a misleading 'breach' until tag::major_incident is cleared — dedup on latest tag state before computing SLAs.

new_ucs.title: DORA Art.24 — Digital operational-resilience testing: test-plan execution attestation
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT assess the quality of the test (not a TLPT scope tool); attests that the annual testing programme executed on time and the per-test evidence is present.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'dora_art24_testing_attest' (daily) emits per test: test_id, test_type, scope_service, planned_date, actual_run_date, pass_fail, finding_count, evidence_complete (boolean), to the compliance_summary index.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.24 requires an established digital operational-resilience testing programme; evidencing that the plan executed with per-test evidence is the direct control output.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.29 Information security during disruption — related; DR scope overlaps with the testing programme.
NIST 800-53 CA-8 Penetration Testing — contributing evidence for the penetration-test subset of the plan.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Removing a planned test's execution record from index=testing past its planned_date+14d causes the UC to flag evidence_complete=0 on the next daily run.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A test with a recorded actual_run_time within the tolerance window reports evidence_complete=1, on_time=1.

new_ucs.dataSources: `index=testing` sourcetype=test:plan_execution (BAS/TLPT/DR runs); `index=ticketing` sourcetype=jira:issue (findings tickets); test-plan register `dora_test_plan.csv`.
new_ucs.spl:
| inputlookup dora_test_plan.csv
| eval planned_date_et=strptime(planned_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| join type=outer test_id
  [search index=testing sourcetype=test:plan_execution
   | stats latest(_time) AS actual_run_time latest(pass_fail) AS pass_fail sum(finding_count) AS finding_count BY test_id]
| eval evidence_complete=if(isnotnull(actual_run_time),1,0)
| eval on_time=if(actual_run_time<=planned_date_et+86400*14,1,0)
| table test_id test_type scope_service planned_date actual_run_time pass_fail finding_count evidence_complete on_time
new_ucs.description: Pivots the DORA test-plan register against the actual execution telemetry from the BAS/TLPT/DR tooling. Each planned test either has matching execution evidence within a 14-day tolerance or it does not — the latter is a direct Art.24 non-conformance.
new_ucs.value: Gives the CISO and the ICT risk committee a single-query attestation that the operational-resilience testing programme executed as planned, replacing an email-based reconciliation that typically slips by weeks.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Maintain dora_test_plan.csv as the approved annual programme; (2) normalise execution output from BAS/DR tools to sourcetype=test:plan_execution; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) evidence_complete=0 past planned_date + 14d escalates to CISO; (5) per-quarter report to Board; (6) missing TLPT for significant entities escalates immediately.
new_ucs.visualization: Calendar heatmap of test executions, bar chart of findings per scope service, single value 'tests past due'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Tests rescheduled due to change freeze produce a legitimate gap — record the rescheduled_date and rebase the 14d tolerance.

new_ucs.title: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 6.1 — Risk-assessment evidence: live risk register decay
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the qualitative content of the risk assessment; surfaces risks whose last_reviewed timestamp exceeds the ISMS-defined review cadence.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'iso_27001_6_1_risk_decay' (daily) outputs per risk: risk_id, category, owner, last_reviewed, days_overdue, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Clause 6.1 requires planning to address risks and opportunities; measurable cadence adherence is the direct evidence.
NIST 800-53 PM-9 Risk Management Strategy — register review cadence is one component of the strategy's operation.
DORA Art.6 — the same register is commonly reused by financial entities; review cadence is one maintenance dimension.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Setting a test risk's last_reviewed to now()-cadence_days-7d causes the UC to surface it with days_overdue=7.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A risk reviewed within its cadence window is absent from the output.

new_ucs.dataSources: `index=grc` sourcetype=archer:risk OR sourcetype=servicenow:grc_risk; ISMS cadence lookup `iso_27001_risk_cadence.csv` (per category, review frequency in days).
new_ucs.app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow
new_ucs.spl:
index=grc sourcetype IN (archer:risk,servicenow:grc_risk) earliest=-1d
| stats latest(last_reviewed) AS last_reviewed latest(category) AS category latest(owner) AS owner BY risk_id
| lookup iso_27001_risk_cadence.csv category OUTPUT cadence_days
| eval days_overdue=round((now()-last_reviewed)/86400,0)-cadence_days
| where days_overdue>0
| table risk_id category owner last_reviewed cadence_days days_overdue
new_ucs.description: Continuously measures how many risks in the register are past their per-category review cadence. Clause 6.1 requires planning that 'addresses risks and opportunities'; a register that has decayed is not planning.
new_ucs.value: Produces the evidence the external auditor expects at stage-2: proof that each risk line in the ISMS register was reviewed within its cadence, identifying exact owners of overdue entries.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Export GRC risk register daily to Splunk; (2) maintain iso_27001_risk_cadence.csv by risk category; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) overdue > 0 opens a task to the risk owner; (5) Board quarterly report uses the rolling 90-day mean of overdue risks.
new_ucs.visualization: Histogram of days_overdue by category, table of top-overdue risks, single value 'total risks overdue today'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Category cadence changes introduced via ISMS committee need a 24h propagation grace — suppress new overdue hits for 24h after the lookup change.

new_ucs.title: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 6.2 — Information-security objectives: measurable-target attainment
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT synthesise objectives; consumes the objectives the ISMS committee has set and reports current attainment against the numeric target for each.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'iso_27001_6_2_objective_attainment' (daily) emits per objective: objective_id, target, current, attainment_pct, trend_7d, status, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Clause 6.2 requires information-security objectives to be measurable; live attainment against the documented target is the direct evidence.
Clause 9.1 (monitoring, measurement, analysis, evaluation) — same measurements feed it.
NIST 800-53 PM-6 Measures of Performance — contributes evidence.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An objective whose current value is below its target returns status=below_target in the next daily run.
controlTest.negativeScenario: An objective meeting its target returns status=met.

new_ucs.dataSources: `index=compliance` sourcetype=isms:objective_result (push from ISMS KPIs); objective targets lookup `iso_27001_objectives.csv`.
new_ucs.spl:
| inputlookup iso_27001_objectives.csv
| join type=outer objective_id
  [search index=compliance sourcetype=isms:objective_result earliest=-30d
   | stats latest(value) AS current latest(_time) AS measured_at BY objective_id
   | eval measured_at=strftime(measured_at,"%Y-%m-%d")]
| eval attainment_pct=round(100*current/target,1)
| eval status=if(attainment_pct>=100,"met","below_target")
| table objective_id target current attainment_pct measured_at status
new_ucs.description: Aligns every Clause 6.2 numeric objective (e.g., 'mean-time-to-patch <30d', 'phishing click rate <3%', 'privileged-session-recording coverage >95%') against its live value. The table is the quarterly Board report.
new_ucs.value: Replaces the manual spreadsheet most ISMS programmes use to report objective attainment. Auditors see the live measurement, the target, and the last update in a single reproducible query.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Push each KPI to index=compliance nightly with objective_id tag; (2) maintain iso_27001_objectives.csv as the ISMS-committee-approved target list; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) status=below_target opens an ISMS action; (5) quarterly Board pack exports the table.
new_ucs.visualization: Bullet chart of attainment % vs target, line chart of 30-day trend per objective, single value 'objectives below target'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Objective targets revised by the ISMS committee within the look-back window create an apparent regression — use the effective-from column in the lookup to compute status.

new_ucs.title: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 8.2 — Operational risk-assessment: per-change risk-score recalculation
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT replace the architect's design review; detects only that a material change (asset scope, data classification, exposure) occurred without a corresponding risk-score recalculation within 7 days.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'iso_27001_8_2_change_risk_recalc' (daily) outputs per change: change_id, asset, old_score, new_score, delta, recalc_performed, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Clause 8.2 Information security risk assessment at planned intervals or on significant change — directly evidenced.
Clause 6.1 Actions to address risks and opportunities — related.
NIST 800-53 RA-3 Risk Assessment — contributes.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A closed change whose linked risk_id shows no change in score within 7d sets recalc_performed=0 and appears in the report.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A change followed by a score update within 7d is absent.

new_ucs.app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow
new_ucs.spl:
index=change sourcetype=servicenow:change state=closed earliest=-14d
| rename affected_ci AS asset
| lookup iso_27001_change_to_risk.csv asset OUTPUT risk_id
| join type=outer risk_id
  [search index=grc sourcetype=archer:risk_score earliest=-14d
   | stats latest(score) AS new_score earliest(score) AS old_score latest(_time) AS recalc_time BY risk_id]
| eval delta=new_score-old_score
| eval recalc_performed=if(recalc_time>=_time AND recalc_time<=_time+604800,1,0)
| where recalc_performed=0 AND abs(delta)>0
| table change_id asset risk_id old_score new_score delta recalc_performed
new_ucs.description: Joins closed changes against their linked risk-register entries and asserts that a score recalculation was produced within 7 days. Clause 8.2 requires the organisation to perform information-security risk assessments 'at planned intervals or when significant changes are proposed' — this UC is the 'significant change' trigger.
new_ucs.value: Gives Clause 8.2 an operational verification signal instead of an annual narrative. Audit objection 'where is the evidence the risk was reassessed after the 2024-Q3 PCI change?' becomes a one-query answer.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Link every CMDB change to affected risks in iso_27001_change_to_risk.csv; (2) normalise Archer risk_score events; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) recalc_performed=0 opens an ISMS action to the risk owner; (5) monthly report to risk committee.
new_ucs.visualization: Scatter of change impact vs recalc latency, table of changes missing recalc, single value 'changes in 14d missing recalc'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Changes categorised 'standard' (no risk implication) should be excluded — filter on change_category!='standard' in the base search.

new_ucs.title: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 9.1 — Monitoring programme coverage: KPI telemetry uptime
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate KPI correctness; detects KPIs whose data pipeline has been silent beyond their contracted freshness window, which means the monitoring programme itself has decayed.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'iso_27001_9_1_kpi_uptime' (every 15m) emits per KPI: kpi_id, last_seen, sla_minutes, is_stale, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Clause 9.1 requires monitoring, measurement, analysis, and evaluation; proof the telemetry is alive is a prerequisite for the rest of the clause.
NIST 800-53 CA-7 Continuous Monitoring — contributing evidence.
SOC 2 CC7.1 System Operations Monitoring — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Stopping the data-engineering job for kpi_id=patch_mttr for 1h past its sla_minutes sets is_stale=1.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A KPI posting within sla_minutes is absent.

new_ucs.dataSources: `index=_internal` sourcetype=splunkd (scheduler logs); `index=compliance` sourcetype=isms:objective_result; KPI freshness lookup `iso_27001_kpi_freshness.csv`.
new_ucs.spl:
| inputlookup iso_27001_kpi_freshness.csv
| join type=outer kpi_id
  [search index=compliance sourcetype=isms:objective_result earliest=-7d
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_seen BY kpi_id]
| eval age_min=round((now()-last_seen)/60,0)
| eval is_stale=if(age_min>sla_minutes OR isnull(last_seen),1,0)
| table kpi_id last_seen age_min sla_minutes is_stale
new_ucs.description: For every ISMS KPI the committee has agreed to measure under Clause 9.1, check the pipeline is alive within the KPI's freshness SLA. Stale pipelines are a silent failure mode of monitoring programmes.
new_ucs.value: Prevents the worst external-audit outcome (monitoring 'was in place' but had silently died) and gives the Head of Platform a single dashboard tile showing ISMS telemetry health.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Push each KPI into index=compliance; (2) maintain iso_27001_kpi_freshness.csv per KPI with its SLA; (3) schedule UC every 15m; (4) is_stale=1 pages the data-engineering owner of the KPI; (5) weekly ISMS report attaches the latest state.
new_ucs.visualization: Heatmap of KPI staleness, table of stale KPIs, single value 'stale KPIs right now'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: KPI suspended during maintenance windows legitimately shows as stale — exclude with a maintenance_calendar.csv lookup.

new_ucs.title: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 9.2 — Internal audit coverage: control sample rotation
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT perform the audit; attests that the internal-audit programme has exercised at least N% of ISMS controls over the rolling year and that no control was audited twice while another was never audited.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'iso_27001_9_2_audit_rotation' (weekly) emits: control_id, last_audited, audit_count_365d, due_for_audit, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Clause 9.2 requires an internal-audit programme exercised at planned intervals; rotation evidence directly satisfies the planned-interval requirement.
SOC 2 CC4.1 — monitoring effectiveness of controls, contributing.
NIST 800-53 CA-2 Control Assessments — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A catalog control with no audit_test events in 365d returns due_for_audit=1 in the weekly run.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A control audited within the window is absent from the overdue table.

new_ucs.dataSources: `index=grc` sourcetype=archer:audit_test OR sourcetype=servicenow:audit_finding; ISMS control catalogue `iso_27001_control_catalog.csv`.
new_ucs.spl:
| inputlookup iso_27001_control_catalog.csv
| join type=outer control_id
  [search index=grc sourcetype IN (archer:audit_test,servicenow:audit_finding) earliest=-365d
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_audited count AS audit_count_365d BY control_id]
| eval days_since=round((now()-last_audited)/86400,0)
| eval due_for_audit=if(isnull(last_audited) OR days_since>365,1,0)
| table control_id category last_audited days_since audit_count_365d due_for_audit
new_ucs.description: Joins the ISMS control catalogue with internal-audit test records over the trailing 365 days. The output tells the Audit Committee which controls are overdue.
new_ucs.value: Closes the typical gap where internal audit over-samples 'hot' controls and silently skips administrative ones. The rotation evidence is what ISO 9.2 requires and what the registrar looks for.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Maintain iso_27001_control_catalog.csv; (2) require internal-audit output to index=grc with control_id tag; (3) schedule UC weekly; (4) due_for_audit=1 adds the control to the next quarter audit plan; (5) annual Audit Committee report.
new_ucs.visualization: Stacked bar of audit count by category, table of overdue controls, single value 'controls never audited'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Controls retired in-year but still in catalog produce false 'overdue' entries — mark retired=true in the lookup.

new_ucs.title: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Annex A.5.24 — Incident-management planning: runbook currency attestation
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT rate runbook quality; detects runbooks past their review cadence or never reviewed.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'iso_27001_a_5_24_runbook_currency' (daily) emits: runbook_id, last_reviewed, cadence_days, is_stale, owner, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.5.24 Information security incident-management planning and preparation — runbook recency is a direct prerequisite.
DORA Art.17 incident-management process — contributes.
NIST 800-53 IR-4 Incident Handling — contributes.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Setting a runbook's last_reviewed to now()-cadence_days-1d returns is_stale=1.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A runbook reviewed within its cadence is not in the output.

new_ucs.dataSources: `index=soar` sourcetype=phantom:playbook OR sourcetype=runbook:metadata; runbook register lookup `ir_runbook_register.csv`.
new_ucs.spl:
| inputlookup ir_runbook_register.csv
| eval last_reviewed=strptime(last_reviewed,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval age_days=round((now()-last_reviewed)/86400,0)
| eval is_stale=if(age_days>cadence_days,1,0)
| where is_stale=1
| table runbook_id owner last_reviewed age_days cadence_days is_stale
new_ucs.description: Flags runbooks past their configured review cadence so the IR team can recertify them before an incident proves they are stale.
new_ucs.value: Satisfies the A.5.24 'planning and preparation' sub-requirement: incident procedures are continuously refreshed, not one-time written.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Maintain ir_runbook_register.csv with owner + cadence_days; (2) schedule UC daily; (3) stale runbooks open a recertification task to the owner with 14d SLA; (4) monthly IR committee review.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of stale runbooks by owner, single value 'stale runbooks', bar chart of age by runbook family.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Runbooks under active revision have an incomplete last_reviewed — exclude where state=in_review.

new_ucs.title: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Annex A.5.25 — Event classification decisions: SIEM-to-incident triage traceability
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT judge whether the classification was correct; asserts that every high-severity SIEM alert has a documented classification decision within the configured SLA.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'iso_27001_a_5_25_event_classification' (every 10m) emits: alert_id, severity, classified_at, decision, decision_sla_met, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.5.25 Assessment and decision on information security events — directly measured by this UC.
SOC 2 CC7.3 Evaluated events and incidents — aligns.
DORA Art.17 — contributes to the classification step of the incident lifecycle.
NIST 800-53 IR-4 Incident Handling — contributes.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A high-severity notable with no phantom:incident record within 1h returns decision_sla_met=0.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A critical alert followed by a classification decision within 30 minutes is absent.

new_ucs.spl:
search `notable` severity IN ("high","critical") earliest=-7d
| rename event_id AS alert_id
| join type=outer alert_id
  [search index=soar sourcetype=phantom:incident earliest=-7d
   | stats latest(_time) AS classified_at latest(decision) AS decision BY alert_id]
| eval decision_sla_met=if(classified_at<=_time+3600,1,0)
| where isnull(decision) OR decision_sla_met=0
| table alert_id severity _time classified_at decision decision_sla_met
new_ucs.description: Measures whether every high/critical SIEM alert received a classification decision (incident / false-positive / suppressed) within the 1h SLA implied by A.5.25.
new_ucs.value: Gives the Audit Committee a single number: 'classification SLA adherence %'. Analysts know their backlog, auditors see the proof.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Ensure SOAR writes classification decisions with alert_id tag; (2) schedule UC every 10m; (3) SLA miss escalates to shift lead; (4) daily SOC report of adherence %; (5) monthly to Audit Committee.
new_ucs.visualization: Funnel from total alerts → classified → on-time, line chart of adherence %, single value 'open unclassified alerts'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Alerts suppressed by tuning rules do not need classification decisions — filter out suppression_reason IS NOT NULL.

new_ucs.title: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 7.2 — Competence evidence: role-based training completion
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT assess training effectiveness; attests that role-required modules are completed within policy cadence by each person in the role.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'iso_27001_7_2_competence' (daily) emits per person: user, role, required_modules, completed_modules, compliance_pct, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Clause 7.2 Competence — directly evidenced by role-based module completion.
HIPAA Security §164.308(a)(5) Security Awareness and Training — contributes.
PCI-DSS 12.6 Security awareness programme — contributes.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A user missing a required module shows compliance_pct<100 in the next daily run.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A user with all required modules completed within cadence is absent.

new_ucs.spl:
index=hr sourcetype=hr:role_assignment earliest=-30d
| stats latest(role) AS role BY user
| lookup iso_27001_training_matrix.csv role OUTPUT required_modules
| eval required_modules=split(required_modules,",")
| join user
  [search index=hr sourcetype=lms:completion status=completed earliest=-365d
   | stats values(module) AS completed_modules BY user]
| eval covered=mvmap(required_modules, if(isnotnull(mvfind(completed_modules,x)),1,0))
| eval compliance_pct=round(100*sum(covered)/mvcount(required_modules),0)
| where compliance_pct<100
| table user role required_modules completed_modules compliance_pct
new_ucs.description: Joins the role-to-module matrix against LMS completions to report each person's coverage. Clause 7.2 requires the organisation to determine and maintain competence.
new_ucs.value: Replaces the annual LMS export with a daily compliance signal. HR can notify managers proactively rather than after the audit finding.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Onboard LMS completions; (2) maintain iso_27001_training_matrix.csv per role; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) compliance_pct<100 triggers an HR email; (5) quarterly report to ISMS committee.
new_ucs.visualization: Stacked bar by role, table of sub-100% users, single value 'users below 100%'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: New hires in their grace window (30d) appear as non-compliant — exclude by hire_date.

new_ucs.title: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 7.5 — Documented information control: policy register approval trail
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT edit policies; detects policy register entries missing an approval record or past their review cadence.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'iso_27001_7_5_policy_trail' (weekly) emits per policy: policy_id, version, approved_by, approved_at, days_to_next_review, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Clause 7.5 Documented information — approval trail + cadence adherence directly evidenced.
SOC 2 CC1.4 — contributes to commitment-and-accountability evidence.
NIST 800-53 PL-2 System Security Plan — contributes.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Setting a policy's approval to now()-cadence_days+29d returns days_to_next_review=1 (near-expiry).
controlTest.negativeScenario: A policy with a recent approval and 200 days remaining is absent.

new_ucs.dataSources: `index=grc` sourcetype=policyhub:policy (change of state); policy cadence lookup `iso_27001_policy_cadence.csv`.
new_ucs.spl:
index=grc sourcetype=policyhub:policy action=approved earliest=-400d
| stats latest(_time) AS approved_at latest(version) AS version latest(approved_by) AS approved_by BY policy_id
| lookup iso_27001_policy_cadence.csv policy_id OUTPUT cadence_days owner
| eval days_to_next_review=cadence_days-round((now()-approved_at)/86400,0)
| where days_to_next_review<=30 OR isnull(approved_at)
| table policy_id version approved_by approved_at days_to_next_review owner
new_ucs.description: For every policy in the register, produce the last approval record and the time to the next review. Policies within 30 days of expiry or with no approval record are surfaced for action.
new_ucs.value: Prevents the common deficiency of policies that 'used to be' approved but whose approval trail has expired — a direct Clause 7.5 non-conformance.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Route policyhub approval events into index=grc; (2) maintain iso_27001_policy_cadence.csv; (3) schedule UC weekly; (4) days_to_next_review<=30 opens a review task to policy owner; (5) quarterly CISO review.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of near-expiry policies, bar chart by owner, single value 'policies expired'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Newly-issued policies within 30 days of issue appear as near-expiry until the first cadence period completes — exclude if age<30d.

new_ucs.title: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 8.1 — Operational planning: change advisory board (CAB) approval evidence
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT approve or reject changes; attests that 'normal' and 'emergency' changes have the required CAB approval record before implementation.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'iso_27001_8_1_cab_approval' (hourly) emits per change: change_id, type, implemented_at, cab_approved_at, approval_met, approver, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Clause 8.1 Operational planning and control — CAB approval adherence is the direct control signal.
SOX-ITGC ChangeMgmt.Approval — directly evidenced.
SOC 2 CC8.1 — change management — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Closing a change with approval_status='rejected' but state='implemented' returns approval_met=0.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A change approved by a CAB-role user before implementation is absent.

new_ucs.spl:
index=change sourcetype=servicenow:change state IN (implemented,closed) earliest=-7d
| stats latest(type) AS type latest(_time) AS implemented_at latest(approval_status) AS approval_status latest(approver) AS approver latest(approval_date) AS cab_approved_at BY change_id
| lookup cab_roles.csv approver OUTPUT is_authorised_cab
| eval approval_met=if(type="emergency",1,if(approval_status="approved" AND is_authorised_cab=1 AND cab_approved_at<=implemented_at,1,0))
| where approval_met=0
| table change_id type implemented_at cab_approved_at approver approval_met
new_ucs.description: Flags implemented changes that were not approved by an authorised CAB member prior to implementation. Clause 8.1 requires the organisation to plan, implement and control processes to meet information-security requirements — CAB approval is the direct control.
new_ucs.value: Turns the 'post-implementation review' into a pre-emptive signal. The Head of IT Operations sees the breach list within the hour.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Export ServiceNow change events; (2) maintain cab_roles.csv listing authorised approvers; (3) schedule UC hourly; (4) approval_met=0 opens a deficiency record with 7d remediation; (5) monthly Audit Committee report.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of unapproved changes, bar chart of breach count by team, single value 'unapproved changes in last 7d'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Emergency changes legitimately bypass CAB; their follow-up retrospective approval must exist separately — this UC excludes type=emergency by design.

new_ucs.title: SOC 2 CC6.6 — Encryption-in-transit validation: cleartext protocols crossing the trust boundary
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT inspect payload; identifies sessions that negotiated plaintext or deprecated TLS versions crossing the production trust zone.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'soc2_cc6_6_intransit_enc' (every 30m) outputs src, dest, dest_port, protocol, tls_version, is_deprecated, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CC6.6 Logical access security measures protect the boundary — cleartext crossing a trust boundary is the direct failure detected.
PCI-DSS 4.2.1 strong cryptography for transmission of cardholder data across open networks — directly evidenced.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.8.24 Use of cryptography — directly evidenced.
HIPAA Security §164.312(e)(1) Transmission security — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Initiating a deliberate http request across trust zones causes a hit within 30m naming the src/dest/protocol.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A TLS1.2+ session with an approved cipher between two zones is absent.

new_ucs.dataSources: `index=stream` sourcetype=stream:tcp OR sourcetype=stream:http OR sourcetype=stream:tls; trust-zone lookup `trust_zones.csv`.
new_ucs.spl:
index=stream sourcetype=stream:tls earliest=-30m
| rename src_ip AS src dest_ip AS dest
| lookup trust_zones.csv ip=src OUTPUT zone AS src_zone
| lookup trust_zones.csv ip=dest OUTPUT zone AS dest_zone
| where src_zone!=dest_zone
| eval is_deprecated=if(tls_version IN ("SSLv3","TLS1.0","TLS1.1"),1,0)
| where is_deprecated=1 OR protocol IN ("http","ftp","telnet","smtp")
| stats count BY src dest dest_port protocol tls_version is_deprecated
new_ucs.description: Detects sessions crossing trust-zone boundaries that negotiated plaintext or deprecated TLS (pre-TLS1.2). Under CC6.6 the service organisation must 'implement logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries'; plaintext at the boundary is the failure mode.
new_ucs.value: Replaces annual TLS-compliance scans with a continuous signal, so drift (a new container accidentally exposing http) is caught within 30 minutes.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Onboard Splunk Stream TLS data; (2) maintain trust_zones.csv; (3) schedule UC every 30m; (4) each hit opens a Platform ticket with src/dest/dest_port; (5) SLA: remediate within 24h.
new_ucs.visualization: Sankey of src_zone → dest_zone by protocol, table of deprecated sessions, single value 'deprecated TLS sessions right now'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Explicit plaintext management LANs (backup target, ILO) are allowlisted in trust_zones.csv via category='management-cleartext-allowed'.

new_ucs.title: SOC 2 CC6.7 — System boundary & data-transmission control: unapproved egress destinations
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT block traffic; detects outbound flows whose destination ASN/country is absent from the approved-egress registry for the originating service.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'soc2_cc6_7_egress_boundary' (every 15m) outputs src_service, dest_ip, dest_asn, dest_country, approved, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CC6.7 Information transmission and disposal — unapproved egress is the direct failure.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.8.23 Web filtering — contributing.
PCI-DSS 1.3.1 traffic to and from the CDE — contributing.
DORA Art.7 — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An outbound flow from the payments service to an ASN not in approved_egress.csv is surfaced within 15m.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A flow to an approved ASN+country tuple is absent.

new_ucs.dataSources: `index=netfw` sourcetype=pan:traffic; ASN/country enrichment via `asn_country.csv`; approved-egress registry `approved_egress.csv`.
new_ucs.app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
new_ucs.spl:
index=netfw sourcetype=pan:traffic action=allow direction=outbound earliest=-15m
| rename src AS src_ip dest AS dest_ip
| lookup service_by_src.csv src_ip OUTPUT src_service
| lookup asn_country.csv ip=dest_ip OUTPUT asn AS dest_asn country AS dest_country
| lookup approved_egress.csv src_service dest_asn OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved) OR approved!="yes"
| stats count sum(bytes_out) AS bytes BY src_service dest_ip dest_asn dest_country
new_ucs.description: Evaluates outbound flows against the per-service approved-egress registry. A flow to an unapproved ASN or country is a direct CC6.7 boundary violation.
new_ucs.value: Provides the evidence SOC 2 auditors want: proof that outbound flows are governed and deviations are captured. Supports reduction of shadow SaaS risk.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Enrich Palo Alto traffic with service/ASN/country; (2) maintain approved_egress.csv; (3) schedule UC every 15m; (4) hit opens a ticket to service owner; (5) 48h SLA; (6) weekly review by Platform team.
new_ucs.visualization: Geo map of unapproved destinations, bar chart by service, single value 'unapproved egress flows (1h)'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Public CDNs and package mirrors that use anycast may produce transient country churn — allowlist by ASN in approved_egress.csv.

new_ucs.title: SOC 2 CC7.1 — System-operations monitoring: uptime attestation and alert-noise governance
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT redesign the monitoring stack; reports coverage (systems with heartbeat) + noise (alerts per system) so the monitoring programme is evaluable.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'soc2_cc7_1_monitoring_ledger' (daily) emits per system: ci_id, heartbeat_last_seen, alerts_24h, coverage_status, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CC7.1 System monitoring for anomalies — coverage + noise are the two programme health metrics.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 8.1 Operational planning and control — contributing.
NIST 800-53 SI-4 System Monitoring — contributing.
DORA Art.10 Detection mechanisms — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Stopping the heartbeat agent on a test CI for 31 minutes sets coverage_status='stale'.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A CI heartbeating within 30m and with alerts_24h<5 reports healthy.

new_ucs.spl:
| inputlookup cmdb_ci_list.csv
| join type=outer ci_id
  [search index=monitoring sourcetype=heartbeat:agent earliest=-1d
   | stats latest(_time) AS heartbeat_last_seen BY ci_id]
| join type=outer ci_id
  [search index=alerts sourcetype=opsgenie:alert earliest=-1d
   | stats count AS alerts_24h BY ci_id]
| eval coverage_status=case(isnull(heartbeat_last_seen),"no-heartbeat",heartbeat_last_seen<now()-1800,"stale",true(),"healthy")
| table ci_id heartbeat_last_seen alerts_24h coverage_status
new_ucs.description: Pivots the CMDB CI list against live heartbeat and alert telemetry to measure which systems are monitored and how noisy they are. CC7.1 requires the entity to 'detect the occurrence of anomalies'; unheartbeated systems cannot be monitored.
new_ucs.value: Lets the Head of IT Operations present the Audit Committee with coverage % (target 100% for production) and alert-noise % (target <5/day/CI). The same query replaces three spreadsheets.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Nightly CMDB sync; (2) heartbeat agent installed on every CI; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) no-heartbeat CIs open a task to IT Ops; (5) quarterly Audit Committee report.
new_ucs.visualization: Donut of coverage_status, top-10 noisiest CIs, single value 'coverage %'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Decommissioned CIs legitimately stop heartbeats — mark retired=true in CMDB lookup.

new_ucs.title: SOC 2 CC7.3 — Evaluated events: threshold breaches without documented rationale
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT auto-classify events; identifies threshold breaches that closed without an analyst rationale field populated.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'soc2_cc7_3_rationale_missing' (hourly) emits per alert: alert_id, severity, rationale_present, closed_at, analyst, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CC7.3 Evaluated events and incidents — rationale adherence is the direct signal.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.25 Event assessment — contributing.
DORA Art.17 Incident management process — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Closing an ES notable via SOAR with empty rationale returns rationale_present=0 on the next hour.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A close with a populated rationale >20 characters is absent.

new_ucs.spl:
search `notable` severity IN ("medium","high","critical") earliest=-7d
| rename event_id AS alert_id
| join type=outer alert_id
  [search index=soar sourcetype=phantom:incident state=closed earliest=-7d
   | stats latest(closed_at) AS closed_at latest(analyst) AS analyst latest(rationale) AS rationale BY alert_id]
| eval rationale_present=if(len(rationale)>20,1,0)
| where isnotnull(closed_at) AND rationale_present=0
| table alert_id severity closed_at analyst rationale_present
new_ucs.description: CC7.3 requires the entity to evaluate events and incidents. Closing a threshold-breach event without a rationale field is a direct control failure.
new_ucs.value: Gives leadership proof that events are not only closed but justifiably closed. Auditors consider an 'auto-closed without rationale' event a material weakness.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Enforce rationale field on SOAR close action; (2) schedule UC hourly; (3) rationale_present=0 reopens the alert for review; (4) weekly SOC report.
new_ucs.visualization: Stacked bar of alerts by analyst × rationale_present, single value 'closed-without-rationale'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Alerts closed by automation rules must use rationale='auto-closed:<rule_id>' rather than blank — reject blank in SOAR.

new_ucs.title: SOC 2 CC7.4 — Incident response: post-incident review completion SLA
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT judge PIR content; asserts the PIR record exists with required fields within the policy-defined window after incident close.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'soc2_cc7_4_pir_sla' (daily) emits per incident: incident_id, closed_at, pir_completed_at, pir_sla_met, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CC7.4 Incident response with post-incident review — SLA adherence is the direct control signal.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.26 Response to information security incidents — contributing.
DORA Art.17 Incident management process — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An incident closed 8 days ago with no PIR record returns pir_sla_met=0.
controlTest.negativeScenario: An incident closed 3 days ago with a PIR created 4 days ago returns pir_sla_met=1.

new_ucs.spl:
index=soar sourcetype=phantom:incident state=closed earliest=-30d
| stats latest(closed_at) AS closed_at BY incident_id
| join type=outer incident_id
  [search index=ticketing sourcetype=jira:issue project=PIR earliest=-60d
   | stats latest(_time) AS pir_completed_at BY incident_id]
| eval pir_sla_met=if(isnotnull(pir_completed_at) AND pir_completed_at<=closed_at+604800,1,0)
| where pir_sla_met=0
| table incident_id closed_at pir_completed_at pir_sla_met
new_ucs.description: CC7.4 requires the entity to 'respond, including by establishing a post-incident review'. This UC measures 7-day PIR completion SLA per closed incident.
new_ucs.value: Surfaces PIR backlog before it becomes an audit finding. Board sees a rolling '% of PIRs on time' metric.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Ensure PIR Jira project is populated; (2) schedule UC daily; (3) pir_sla_met=0 opens a reminder ticket; (4) weekly SOC report; (5) quarterly Board report.
new_ucs.visualization: Waterfall of incidents → PIR completed → on time, single value 'open incidents overdue PIR'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Incidents reopened and later re-closed legitimately extend the window — use latest(closed_at).

new_ucs.title: SOC 2 CC1.1 — Integrity and ethical values: code-of-conduct acknowledgement trail
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT test the substance of the code of conduct; attests that every active employee has acknowledged the current version within policy cadence.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'soc2_cc1_1_coc_ack' (daily) emits per employee: user, hire_date, latest_ack_version, latest_ack_date, is_compliant, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CC1.1 Integrity and ethical values — acknowledgement coverage is the direct evidence.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 6.3 Competence — contributing; the CoC is one training module.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A user with no CoC acknowledgement in 13 months returns is_compliant=0.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A user who acknowledged the current version within the year is absent.

new_ucs.spl:
index=hr sourcetype=hr:employee status=active earliest=-30d
| stats latest(hire_date) AS hire_date BY user
| join user type=outer
  [search index=hr sourcetype=lms:acknowledgement module=code_of_conduct earliest=-2y
   | stats latest(version) AS latest_ack_version latest(_time) AS latest_ack_date BY user]
| lookup coc_versions.csv AS latest OUTPUT current_version
| eval is_compliant=if(latest_ack_version==current_version AND latest_ack_date>now()-31536000,1,0)
| where is_compliant=0
| table user hire_date latest_ack_version latest_ack_date is_compliant
new_ucs.description: Surfaces active employees who have not acknowledged the current code-of-conduct version within the last 12 months.
new_ucs.value: Removes the audit finding 'CoC acknowledgement trail is incomplete' by making HR's coverage measurable daily.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Nightly HR roster sync; (2) maintain coc_versions.csv; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) is_compliant=0 triggers an HR reminder email; (5) quarterly compliance report.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of non-acknowledging users, bar chart by department, single value 'non-compliant users'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Employees within their 30-day grace window are excluded via hire_date.

new_ucs.title: SOC 2 CC9.1 — Risk-mitigation activity: vendor-risk action closure SLA
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT rate vendors; measures closure SLA for risk-mitigation actions from vendor assessments.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'soc2_cc9_1_vendor_risk_sla' (daily) emits per action: action_id, vendor, severity, due_at, closed_at, sla_met, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CC9.1 Risk mitigation — vendor-action closure is the direct evidence.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.19 Information security in supplier relationships — contributing.
DORA Art.28 ICT third-party risk — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A high-severity action past due_at with state=open returns sla_met=0.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A closed action that was closed before due_at is absent.

new_ucs.spl:
index=grc sourcetype=servicenow:vendor_risk state IN (open,closed) earliest=-120d
| stats latest(state) AS state latest(severity) AS severity latest(due_at) AS due_at latest(closed_at) AS closed_at BY action_id vendor
| eval sla_met=case(state="closed" AND closed_at<=due_at,1,state="closed",0,now()>due_at,0,true(),1)
| where sla_met=0
| table action_id vendor severity due_at closed_at sla_met

new_ucs.title: SOC 2 P1.1 — Privacy notice: consent-record freshness for privacy-notice version changes

new_ucs.spl:
index=consent sourcetype=onetrust:consent earliest=-14d
| stats latest(consent_version) AS consent_version latest(_time) AS consent_time BY subject_id
| lookup privacy_notice_versions.csv AS latest OUTPUT current_version
| eval days_stale=round((now()-consent_time)/86400,0)
| eval needs_refresh=if(consent_version!=current_version,1,0)
| where needs_refresh=1
| table subject_id consent_version current_version days_stale needs_refresh

index=netfw sourcetype IN (pan:traffic,cisco:asa) action=allow earliest=-15m

| where (src_zone="CDE" AND dest_zone="UNTRUSTED") OR (src_zone="UNTRUSTED" AND dest_zone="CDE")
| lookup pci_approved_flows.csv src_zone dest_zone dest_port protocol OUTPUT approved
| where approved!="yes" OR isnull(approved)
| stats count BY src src_zone dest dest_zone dest_port protocol approved

new_ucs.spl:
index=configmgmt sourcetype IN (chef:compliance,ansible:callback) earliest=-30m
| stats latest(role) AS role latest(baseline_version) AS baseline_version latest(resource_failed) AS resource_failed latest(_time) AS last_converged BY hostname
| eval drift_count=coalesce(resource_failed,0)
| where drift_count>0 OR last_converged<now()-86400
| table hostname role baseline_version drift_count last_converged

new_ucs.dataSources: `index=app` sourcetypes from payment applications; `index=web` access logs; redaction-pipeline attestation events.
new_ucs.spl:
index=app OR index=web earliest=-10m
| eval pan_match=if(match(_raw,"(?<![0-9])(?:4[0-9]{12}(?:[0-9]{3})?|5[1-5][0-9]{14}|3[47][0-9]{13})(?![0-9])"),1,0)
| eval cvv_match=if(match(_raw,"(?i)cvv=[0-9]{3,4}"),1,0)
| where pan_match=1 OR cvv_match=1
| eval match_type=case(pan_match=1 AND cvv_match=1,"pan+cvv",pan_match=1,"pan",true(),"cvv")
| stats count BY sourcetype host match_type
new_ucs.description: Detects cleartext PAN or CVV patterns in application / web logs. PCI-DSS 3.3 prohibits storing sensitive authentication data post-authorisation; logs containing such data are a direct non-conformance.
new_ucs.value: Reduces the window between a logging regression introducing a cleartext PAN and the compliance team's discovery from 'next log review' to 10 minutes.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Apply a separate restrictive index for hits (ACL); (2) schedule UC every 10m; (3) hit creates a P1 to DPO + app owner; (4) index-time redaction must be fixed + backfill scrub; (5) monthly DPO report.
new_ucs.visualization: Timechart of match_count, table of top offending sourcetypes, single value 'detections in last hour'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Synthetic test data may look like a PAN — allow-list via the synthetic_test flag in the application.

new_ucs.spl:
index=cicd sourcetype IN (github:actions,gitlab:pipeline) event_type=deployment target_env IN ("prod-cde") earliest=-24h

  [search index=security sourcetype=veracode:scan earliest=-30d

new_ucs.spl:
search (index=windows sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode=4624) OR (index=idp sourcetype=okta:system_log eventType=user.session.start) earliest=-10m
| lookup privileged_accounts.csv user OUTPUT is_privileged
| where is_privileged="yes"
| eval auth_method=case(isnotnull(LogonType) AND LogonType IN ("2","10"),"interactive",isnotnull(factor),factor,true(),"password")
| eval mfa_used=if(auth_method IN ("mfa_push","mfa_totp","webauthn","smartcard","token:hardware"),1,0)
| where mfa_used=0
| table user src dest auth_method mfa_used

new_ucs.implementation: (1) Maintain privileged_accounts.csv; (2) ensure IDP emits factor evidence; (3) schedule UC every 10m; (4) hit opens a P1 and disables the session; (5) monthly CISO review of patterns.
