| where in_cde="yes" AND (abs(offset_ms)>1000 OR last_ntp_sync<now()-3600)
| table hostname offset_ms stratum last_ntp_sync
new_ucs.description: PCI-DSS 10.4 requires time synchronisation across CDE systems. This UC flags drift > 1s or NTP sync gaps > 1h.
new_ucs.value: Prevents the classic forensic nightmare of 'when exactly did the event occur' because the CDE clocks disagreed.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Enforce w32time/chrony logs to Splunk; (2) maintain cde_hosts.csv; (3) schedule UC every 15m; (4) drift opens a Platform ticket; (5) quarterly sampling for auditor.
new_ucs.visualization: Scatter of offset_ms per host, table of drifted hosts, single value 'hosts over 1s drift'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Virtualisation drift during host migration may cause short bursts — smooth with 5-minute averaging before trigger.

new_ucs.title: PCI-DSS 10.6 — Log review: daily-review evidence for CDE data sources
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT review logs; attests a scheduled review artefact exists daily per CDE data source.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'pci_10_6_daily_review' (daily) emits per source: source, reviewer, reviewed_at, artefact_id, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI-DSS 10.6 Logs reviewed — review artefact is the direct evidence.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.28 Collection of evidence — contributing.
SOC 2 CC7.1 — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Removing today's review artefact for the pan:traffic source returns it in the daily output.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A source with a reviewed_at timestamp within the day is absent.

new_ucs.dataSources: `index=compliance` sourcetype=review:daily (from SOC daily review tool); CDE-source list `pci_cde_log_sources.csv`.
new_ucs.spl:
| inputlookup pci_cde_log_sources.csv
| join type=outer source
  [search index=compliance sourcetype=review:daily earliest=-1d
   | stats latest(reviewer) AS reviewer latest(_time) AS reviewed_at latest(artefact_id) AS artefact_id BY source]
| eval review_today=if(reviewed_at>=relative_time(now(),"@d"),1,0)
| where review_today=0
| table source reviewer reviewed_at artefact_id review_today
new_ucs.description: PCI-DSS 10.6 requires daily review of logs. This UC tests the presence of the daily review record — the auditor-visible outcome of the control.
new_ucs.value: Auditors frequently disallow 'we review daily' without evidence. The review artefact lookup gives the evidence.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) SOC tool must write review artefacts to index=compliance; (2) maintain pci_cde_log_sources.csv; (3) schedule UC daily at 23:55 UTC; (4) gap opens a ticket for the next morning's shift; (5) monthly CISO report.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of sources missing review, time chart by day, single value 'days with gaps (30d)'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Automated high-fidelity systems (e.g., PAN unified logging with correlation) may substitute for daily review — allow-list via source_type='automated-attest' in the lookup.

new_ucs.title: PCI-DSS 10.7 — Log retention: CDE data-source retention + immutability attestation
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT change retention; attests (a) configured index retention for CDE indexes ≥ 365d and (b) WORM archive currency.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'pci_10_7_retention_attest' (daily) emits per index: index, configured_days, oldest_event_age_d, worm_last_write, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI-DSS 10.7 Log retention — retention + WORM attestation is directly evidenced.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.33 Protection of records — directly evidenced.
NIST 800-53 AU-11 Audit Record Retention — directly evidenced.
SOX-ITGC Logging.Integrity — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Setting a CDE index frozenTimePeriodInSecs to 180d returns configured_days=180 in the output.
controlTest.negativeScenario: An index with 400d retention + recent WORM heartbeat is absent.

new_ucs.spl:
| rest /services/data/indexes
| where match(title,"^(cde|pci)_")
| eval configured_days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400,0)
| table title configured_days minTime maxTime
| rename title AS index
| eval oldest_event_age_d=round((now()-strptime(minTime,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"))/86400,0)
| join type=outer index
  [search index=compliance sourcetype=worm:signer earliest=-7d
   | stats latest(_time) AS worm_last_write BY index]
| where configured_days<365 OR oldest_event_age_d<365 OR worm_last_write<now()-86400
| table index configured_days oldest_event_age_d worm_last_write
new_ucs.description: Attests the retention and WORM posture required by PCI-DSS 10.7 (one-year retention, three months immediately available).
new_ucs.value: Prevents the audit-killing discovery that retention was silently reduced during an index rebuild. Daily attestation beats the quarterly spot-check.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Name CDE indexes with cde_/pci_ prefix; (2) WORM signer writes per-index heartbeat; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) gap opens a Platform ticket; (5) monthly CISO review.
new_ucs.visualization: Bar of retention days by index, table of gaps, single value 'indexes below 365d'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: New indexes legitimately have oldest_event_age_d<365 until one year passes — mark age_exempt=true in an index-metadata lookup.

new_ucs.title: PCI-DSS 11.3 — Vulnerability programme: overdue scan cadence and unremediated high-severity
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT replace the scanner; measures cadence adherence (quarterly for external, internal as required) and remediation SLA for high/critical findings.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'pci_11_3_scan_sla' (daily) emits per scope: scope, last_scan_at, sla_met, open_high_findings, mean_age_days, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI-DSS 11.3 External and internal vulnerabilities identified — directly evidenced.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.8.8 Management of technical vulnerabilities — directly evidenced.
NIST 800-53 RA-5 Vulnerability Scanning — directly evidenced.
DORA Art.8 ICT risk identification — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A scope whose last_scan_at is 100 days ago with 3 open high findings aged 45 days returns sla_met=0.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A scope scanned within cadence with all findings <30 days old is absent.

new_ucs.spl:
index=vm sourcetype IN (tenable:sc:vuln,qualys:host) earliest=-120d
| stats latest(_time) AS last_scan_at count(eval(severity IN ("high","critical") AND state="open")) AS open_high_findings avg(eval(if(state="open" AND severity IN ("high","critical"),now()-first_seen,null))) AS mean_age_s BY scan_scope
| eval mean_age_days=round(mean_age_s/86400,0)
| lookup pci_scan_scope.csv scan_scope OUTPUT cadence_days sla_days
| eval sla_met=if(last_scan_at>=now()-cadence_days*86400 AND mean_age_days<=sla_days,1,0)
| where sla_met=0
| table scan_scope last_scan_at sla_met open_high_findings mean_age_days
new_ucs.description: Produces, per scan scope, the evidence required by PCI-DSS 11.3 — scans are on cadence and high/critical findings are remediated within SLA.
new_ucs.value: Closes the #1 open-finding reason at QSAs — 'the scan history was incomplete'. Continuous measurement is the intended replacement.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Normalise scanner feeds; (2) maintain pci_scan_scope.csv (quarterly external, per-change internal); (3) schedule UC daily; (4) sla_met=0 opens a P2; (5) quarterly QSA report.
new_ucs.visualization: Bullet chart of SLA adherence by scope, table of overdue scopes, single value 'scopes overdue'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Immaterial findings whose exception was approved in the risk register show as 'open' — tag state='accepted_risk' and exclude.

new_ucs.title: PCI-DSS 11.4 — Intrusion detection: IDS signature/health attestation + untuned alert monitoring
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT review alerts; attests the IDS/IPS sensors are healthy, up-to-date on signatures, and producing events.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'pci_11_4_ids_attest' (every 30m) emits per sensor: sensor, last_seen, sig_version, age_d, state, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI-DSS 11.4 Intrusion detection / prevention — directly evidenced.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.8.16 Monitoring activities — directly evidenced.
SOC 2 CC7.1 — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Disabling a sensor for 40m returns it with state=offline.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A healthy sensor with signatures ≤7d old is absent.

new_ucs.app: Splunk Add-on for Suricata, Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
new_ucs.spl:
| inputlookup pci_ids_sensors.csv
| join type=outer sensor
  [search index=ids earliest=-30m
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_seen latest(sig_version) AS sig_version BY sensor]
| eval age_d=round((now()-strptime(sig_version,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| eval state=case(isnull(last_seen),"offline",last_seen<now()-1800,"stale",age_d>7,"signatures-stale",true(),"healthy")
| where state!="healthy"
| table sensor last_seen sig_version age_d state
new_ucs.description: PCI-DSS 11.4 requires IDS/IPS use. This UC attests the sensors are alive and their signatures are fresh. Sensor gaps are a top-3 audit finding.
new_ucs.value: Transforms annual sign-off into 30-minute ground-truth. IR team sees stale signatures before they become an audit issue.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Maintain pci_ids_sensors.csv; (2) onboard sensor telemetry; (3) schedule UC every 30m; (4) non-healthy state opens a P1/P2; (5) monthly CISO review.
new_ucs.visualization: Donut of state, table of non-healthy sensors, single value 'stale sensors'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Sensor maintenance windows legitimately show 'offline' — allowlist with maintenance_calendar.csv.

new_ucs.title: PCI-DSS 12.10 — Incident response: IR readiness — playbook exercise evidence
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT run the exercise; measures whether the CDE IR playbook has been exercised within policy cadence (annually) and if the exercise produced findings.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'pci_12_10_ir_exercise' (weekly) emits per playbook: playbook_id, scope_cde, last_exercise_at, finding_count, exercise_lead, cadence_met, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI-DSS 12.10 Security incident response — drill cadence is direct evidence.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.24 Incident management planning — contributing.
DORA Art.24 Testing programme — contributing.
SOC 2 CC7.4 — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A CDE playbook with no drill event in 13 months returns cadence_met=0.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A playbook exercised within the year is absent.

new_ucs.spl:
| inputlookup ir_playbook_register.csv
| where scope_cde="yes"
| join type=outer playbook_id
  [search index=testing sourcetype=ir:drill earliest=-14mon
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_exercise_at latest(finding_count) AS finding_count latest(exercise_lead) AS exercise_lead BY playbook_id]
| eval cadence_met=if(last_exercise_at>=now()-365*86400,1,0)
| table playbook_id scope_cde last_exercise_at finding_count exercise_lead cadence_met
new_ucs.description: PCI-DSS 12.10.2 requires IR plan testing at least annually. This UC evidences the test happened per CDE playbook.
new_ucs.value: Converts the 'we did a tabletop' email into indexed evidence auditors can query directly.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) IR exercise tool writes drill events to index=testing; (2) maintain ir_playbook_register.csv; (3) schedule UC weekly; (4) cadence_met=0 schedules a drill; (5) annual Board report.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of playbooks with drill history, bar chart of findings per exercise, single value 'playbooks overdue'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Combined multi-playbook drills may link to one of several playbook_ids — maintain a drill_to_playbook mapping.

new_ucs.title: PCI-DSS 12.3 — Targeted risk analysis: frequency adherence for per-requirement TRAs
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the TRA content; reports TRAs whose refresh is overdue per documented frequency.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'pci_12_3_tra_refresh' (daily) emits per TRA: tra_id, requirement, frequency_days, last_refresh_at, overdue_days, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI-DSS 12.3 Targeted risk analysis — cadence adherence is direct evidence.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 8.2 Information security risk assessment — contributing.
DORA Art.6 ICT risk-management framework — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A TRA with frequency_days=365 and last_refresh_at 400d ago returns overdue_days=35.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A TRA refreshed in the past 200d with frequency_days=365 is absent.

new_ucs.spl:
| inputlookup pci_tra_register.csv
| join type=outer tra_id
  [search index=grc sourcetype=archer:tra_record earliest=-2y
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_refresh_at BY tra_id]
| eval overdue_days=round((now()-last_refresh_at)/86400,0)-frequency_days
| where overdue_days>0 OR isnull(last_refresh_at)
| table tra_id requirement frequency_days last_refresh_at overdue_days
new_ucs.description: PCI-DSS v4.0 introduced targeted risk analyses (TRAs) at 12.3. This UC makes the refresh cadence measurable.
new_ucs.value: Gives compliance an explicit overdue list — the v4.0 TRA is a common reason for 'in progress' at year-two assessments.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Maintain pci_tra_register.csv with requirement and frequency_days; (2) GRC writes each refresh event; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) overdue TRAs open a CISO-owned task; (5) quarterly compliance report.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of overdue TRAs, bar chart by requirement, single value 'TRAs overdue'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: TRAs whose requirement is marked 'not applicable' should be excluded via scope_applicable=false.

new_ucs.title: SOX-ITGC AccessMgmt.Provisioning — Financial-system user provisioning SLA & workflow adherence
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT grant access; measures (a) every add to a financial-system group followed an approved ticket and (b) provisioning happened within the SLA from approval.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'sox_itgc_provisioning' (daily) emits per add: group, user, added_at, ticket_id, approved_at, sla_met, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: SOX-ITGC AccessMgmt.Provisioning — approval + SLA evidence is the direct control output.
SOC 2 CC6.2 Logical access provisioning — contributing.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.18 Access rights — directly evidenced.
PCI-DSS 8.2.4 User provisioning — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Adding a user to a financial-system group with no matching ServiceNow approval returns sla_met=0.
controlTest.negativeScenario: An add that follows an approved ticket within 24h is absent.

new_ucs.app: Splunk Add-on for Okta, Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow
new_ucs.spl:
index=iam eventType=group.user_membership.add earliest=-7d
| lookup financial_systems_groups.csv group OUTPUT in_scope
| where in_scope="yes"
| rename actor AS added_by target.id AS user
| eval added_at=_time
| join type=outer user group
  [search index=ticketing sourcetype=servicenow:request request_type=access_request state=approved earliest=-30d
   | stats latest(ticket_id) AS ticket_id latest(approved_at) AS approved_at BY user target_group
   | rename target_group AS group]
| eval sla_met=if(isnotnull(approved_at) AND added_at<=approved_at+24*3600,1,0)
| where sla_met=0
| table group user added_at ticket_id approved_at sla_met
new_ucs.description: For every add to a financial-system group, assert an approved ServiceNow request exists and provisioning happened within 24h of approval.
new_ucs.value: Closes the #1 SOX deficiency 'access provisioned without an approved ticket'. Internal audit gets a daily report rather than a quarterly spot-check.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Maintain financial_systems_groups.csv; (2) enforce ServiceNow workflow; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) sla_met=0 opens a SOX deficiency record; (5) monthly CFO report.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of provisioning without approval, bar chart by group, single value 'unapproved provisions (7d)'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Break-glass adds via approved emergency-access workflow populate approved_at via a separate sourcetype — ensure the join covers both.

new_ucs.title: SOX-ITGC AccessMgmt.Termination — Deprovisioning SLA after HR termination event
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT deprovision; measures elapsed time between HR termination and removal of all financial-system access.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'sox_itgc_termination_sla' (hourly) emits per terminated user: user, term_date, last_access_revoked, elapsed_hours, sla_met, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: SOX-ITGC AccessMgmt.Termination — revocation SLA is direct evidence.
SOC 2 CC6.3 Logical access removal — directly evidenced.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.18 Access rights — directly evidenced.
PCI-DSS 8.2.5 Terminate access within 1 business day — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A Workday termination not followed by an IAM deactivate event within 24h returns sla_met=0.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A termination with a matching deactivate event within 2h is absent.

new_ucs.app: Splunk Add-on for Okta, Splunk Add-on for Workday
new_ucs.spl:
index=hr sourcetype=workday:termination earliest=-7d
| stats latest(_time) AS term_date BY user
| join user type=outer
  [search index=iam (eventType=user.lifecycle.deactivate OR eventType=group.user_membership.remove) earliest=-7d
   | stats max(_time) AS last_access_revoked BY user]
| eval elapsed_hours=round((last_access_revoked-term_date)/3600,1)
| eval sla_met=if(elapsed_hours<=24 AND isnotnull(last_access_revoked),1,0)
| where sla_met=0
| table user term_date last_access_revoked elapsed_hours sla_met
new_ucs.description: Joins HR termination events against IAM deactivation/removal events to measure whether access was revoked within the 24h SLA implied by SOX-ITGC AccessMgmt.Termination.
new_ucs.value: The most common SOX finding — ex-employees retaining access — becomes measurable hour-by-hour.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Route Workday terminations to Splunk; (2) route Okta lifecycle events; (3) schedule UC hourly; (4) sla_met=0 pages the IAM team; (5) monthly CFO report.
new_ucs.visualization: Timechart of SLA adherence %, table of breaches, single value 'open SLA breaches'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Contractor terminations with delayed HR events may produce a false breach — backfill term_date from contract_end_date in vendor_register.csv.

new_ucs.title: SOX-ITGC ChangeMgmt.Testing — Financial-system change test-evidence completeness
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT judge test quality; attests each financial-system change has test evidence (UAT artefact, attachment or ticket) linked to it before deploy.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'sox_itgc_change_testing' (hourly) emits per change: change_id, system, test_evidence_present, tester, deployed_at, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: SOX-ITGC ChangeMgmt.Testing — test-evidence presence is direct evidence.
SOC 2 CC8.1 Change management — directly evidenced.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.8.32 Change management — directly evidenced.
PCI-DSS 6.4.6 Impact of changes — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Implementing a financial-system change with no UAT subtask returns test_evidence_present=0.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A change with a closed-complete UAT subtask is absent.

new_ucs.spl:
index=change sourcetype=servicenow:change state=implemented earliest=-7d
| lookup financial_systems_apps.csv system OUTPUT in_scope
| where in_scope="yes"
| stats latest(_time) AS deployed_at latest(change_id) AS change_id BY change_id system
| join type=outer change_id
  [search index=change sourcetype=servicenow:change_task task_type=uat state=closed_complete earliest=-60d
   | stats latest(assigned_to) AS tester count AS uat_tasks BY change_id]
| eval test_evidence_present=if(uat_tasks>=1,1,0)
| where test_evidence_present=0
| table change_id system tester deployed_at test_evidence_present
new_ucs.description: For every change to a financial system, assert a closed-complete UAT task exists. SOX-ITGC ChangeMgmt.Testing requires documented test evidence.
new_ucs.value: Converts a common sampling control into full-coverage evidence, reducing audit sampling and cost.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Maintain financial_systems_apps.csv; (2) configure ServiceNow to require a UAT subtask; (3) schedule UC hourly; (4) test_evidence_present=0 triggers a deficiency record.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of missing-test changes, bar chart by system, single value 'missing test evidence'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Automated changes (configuration as code) may use automated test jobs rather than UAT tasks — accept sourcetype=cicd:tests result=pass as equivalent evidence.

new_ucs.title: SOX-ITGC ChangeMgmt.Approval — Segregation of duties in financial-system change approval
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT configure workflow; detects changes where implementer == approver, or approval occurred AFTER implementation.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'sox_itgc_change_approval' (hourly) emits per change: change_id, system, implementer, approver, approval_before_implementation, sod_violation, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: SOX-ITGC ChangeMgmt.Approval — SoD evidence is direct.
SOC 2 CC8.1 Change management — directly evidenced.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.3 Segregation of duties — contributing.
PCI-DSS 6.4.4 Change approvals — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A financial-system change with implementer=approver returns sod_violation=1.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A change with distinct implementer/approver and approval_date<=state_date is absent.

new_ucs.spl:
index=change sourcetype=servicenow:change earliest=-7d
| stats latest(implementer) AS implementer latest(approver) AS approver latest(approval_date) AS approved_at latest(state_date) AS implemented_at BY change_id system
| lookup financial_systems_apps.csv system OUTPUT in_scope
| where in_scope="yes"
| eval sod_violation=if(implementer=approver,1,0)
| eval approval_before_implementation=if(approved_at<=implemented_at,1,0)
| where sod_violation=1 OR approval_before_implementation=0
| table change_id system implementer approver approved_at implemented_at sod_violation approval_before_implementation
new_ucs.description: Detects (a) implementer = approver SoD violations and (b) approvals post-dating implementation.
new_ucs.value: Eliminates the two patterns that SOX auditors most frequently cite. The daily report is the control evidence.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Maintain financial_systems_apps.csv; (2) capture ServiceNow workflow events; (3) schedule UC hourly; (4) violations open an immediate deficiency; (5) monthly CFO report.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of violations, bar chart by system, single value 'SoD + post-implementation approvals'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Standard-pre-approved changes legitimately have matching implementer/approver — filter on type!='standard'.

new_ucs.title: SOX-ITGC Operations.JobSchedule — Batch-schedule monitoring: financial-job exception visibility
new_ucs.exclusions: Does NOT run the jobs; detects financial batch jobs that missed their window, failed, or ran with warnings without operator acknowledgement.
new_ucs.evidence: Saved search 'sox_itgc_batch_jobs' (every 30m) emits per job: job_id, run_at, state, ack_operator, ack_at, ack_met, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: SOX-ITGC Operations.JobSchedule — exception acknowledgement evidence.
SOC 2 CC7.1 — contributing.
ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.8.30 Outsourced development (for outsourced batch) — contributing.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A FAILED financial-job with no batch-ack incident within 4h returns ack_met=0.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A job returning state=OK is absent.

new_ucs.dataSources: `index=batch` sourcetype=autosys:event OR sourcetype=controlm:job; operator ack `index=ticketing` sourcetype=servicenow:incident (category=batch-ack).
new_ucs.spl:
index=batch sourcetype IN (autosys:event,controlm:job) earliest=-30m
| lookup financial_batch_jobs.csv job_id OUTPUT in_scope expected_window
| where in_scope="yes"
| stats latest(state) AS state latest(_time) AS run_at BY job_id
| where state IN ("FAILED","LATE","WARNING")
| join type=outer job_id
  [search index=ticketing sourcetype=servicenow:incident category=batch-ack earliest=-24h
   | stats latest(assigned_to) AS ack_operator latest(_time) AS ack_at BY job_id]
| eval ack_met=if(isnotnull(ack_operator) AND ack_at>=run_at AND ack_at<=run_at+4*3600,1,0)
| where ack_met=0
| table job_id run_at state ack_operator ack_at ack_met
new_ucs.description: Measures per-job batch exceptions and requires operator acknowledgement within 4h. SOX-ITGC Operations.JobSchedule requires exception monitoring for financial batch jobs.
new_ucs.value: Makes the typically-spreadsheet-based batch monitoring queryable — Ops has a live queue, auditors have a history.
new_ucs.implementation: (1) Route Autosys/Control-M events; (2) maintain financial_batch_jobs.csv; (3) schedule UC every 30m; (4) unacked exceptions open an incident; (5) monthly CFO + Head of IT Ops review.
new_ucs.visualization: Table of unacked exceptions, time chart of exception rate, single value 'open batch exceptions'.
new_ucs.knownFalsePositives: Jobs whose expected_window is currently paused (month-end blackout) should be excluded via the lookup's active window.
